title: viscum austriacum subsp. pini
intro: Viscum austriacum subsp. pini, also known as European mistletoe, is a semi-parasitic plant that grows on the branches of pine trees. It has been used in traditional medicine for its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid compounds, particularly viscopine and aucubine. The health benefits of Viscum austriacum subsp. pini include its potential to stimulate the immune system, reduce cancer cell growth, and alleviate symptoms of autoimmune diseases such as rheumatoid arthritis. Viscum austriacum subsp. pini can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts.
